About

Who I Am

I'm a first-year Computer Science student at Hampton University — Dean's List, GPA 4.07, CS GPA 4.1 — focused on AI, machine learning, and software that ships. I graduated from Oxon Hill High School in 2025 in the top 5% of my class with a 4.4 GPA.

I've already had hands-on experience beyond the classroom: I programmed an underwater autonomous vehicle at the Naval Surface Warfare Center, conducted research at the University of Maryland's NES Lab, competed in the ICPC as one of only 6 freshmen selected, and completed training in Python, ML, cloud computing, and HPC at the SGX3 Coding Institute.

This summer I'm joining the Greenwood Project Fintech Academy to go deeper on fintech systems and applied AI. I'm also founding the Black Students in Tech chapter at Hampton — because representation in this field matters.

Outside of code: basketball, violin, painting, baking, and video games.

SGX3 Coding Institute — ML & Cloud

Projects

What I've Built

● In Development — AI

Market Minds

An AI-powered stock market simulation game with realistic, randomized market behavior. Uses a large language model API to generate dynamic news events, earnings shocks, and volatility cycles — making every session unpredictable. Built to teach investing intuition through play.

JavaScript LLM API (Claude) HTML/CSS Financial Modeling
In Development↗
● Java — Hampton University

NutriTrack CLI

Placed 1st out of all Pre-College Program teams at Hampton University. Co-built a Java application that tracks daily meal counts and caloric intake with personalized goal-setting — demonstrated clean OOP design and program architecture to a panel of judges.

JavaCLIOOP
View on GitHub↗
● Java — Hampton University

Wordle Clone

Built a fully playable Wordle clone in Java using lists and loops to manage the game's core mechanics — tracking guesses, validating letters, and handling win/loss conditions. A clean implementation of game logic using fundamental CS concepts.

Java Lists Loops Game Logic
View on GitHub↗
● Hardware — Naval Warfare Center

SeaGlide AUV

Built and programmed an underwater autonomous vehicle at the Naval Surface Warfare Center, Carderock Division. Designed a servo-driven buoyancy engine on an Arduino Pro Mini to control depth and pitch — one of a select cohort of high school interns chosen for the program.

ArduinoC++ Embedded SystemsServo Control
View on GitHub↗
● Java — Competitive Programming

ICPC Solutions

Selected as 1 of only 6 freshmen to represent Hampton University at the International Collegiate Programming Contest. Competed on a 3-person team solving algorithmic problems under strict time limits using Java and data structures.

JavaAlgorithms Data StructuresCompetitive Programming
View on GitHub↗
● Java

Tic-Tac-Toe Engine

Two-player Tic-Tac-Toe with a rule-based AI opponent built in Java. Focused on OOP design, game loop architecture, and win-condition logic — clean, well-commented code written as a readable reference project.

JavaOOPGame Logic
View on GitHub↗
